message:
feat(fnp-ufunc): add datetime_as_string and epoch_days_to_civil for ISO 8601 formatting

Implement numpy.datetime_as_string() parity for converting datetime64 and
timedelta64 arrays to human-readable ISO 8601 string representations:

- epoch_days_to_civil: converts Unix epoch days to (year, month, day)
  using Howard Hinnant's civil_from_days algorithm, correctly handling
  negative epochs (pre-1970 dates) via Euclidean division
- datetime_as_string: formats datetime64 values at configurable precision
  (Day through Nanosecond), with NaT handling and timedelta64 support
- format_datetime_ticks: builds ISO 8601 strings with progressive time
  component inclusion based on the requested unit precision
- format_timedelta_ticks: simple "{ticks} {unit_name}" formatting for
  timedelta64 values

The implementation converts ticks to nanoseconds via i128 intermediate
to avoid overflow, then decomposes into calendar and sub-day components.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
